name: dsd-flyio

metadata.name: dsd-flyio
metadata.summary: A plugin for django-simple-deploy, supporting deployments to Fly.io.

documentation:
# dsd-flyio

A plugin for deploying Django projects to Fly.io, using django-simple-deploy.

For full documentation, see the documentation for [django-simple-deploy](https://django-simple-deploy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

modules.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages
documentation: A collection of messages used in flyio.py.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.region_not_found
documentation: Could not find a region to deploy to.
--- Dynamic strings ---
These need to be generated in functions, to display information that's determined as
the script runs.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.confirm_use_org_name
documentation: Confirm use of this org name to create a new project.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.confirm_create_db
documentation: Confirm it's okay to create a Postgres database on the user's account.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.use_attached_db
documentation: Found the db attached, with only default users and app_name-db user.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.use_unattached_db
documentation: Found the db unattached, with only default users.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.cant_use_db
documentation: Can't use the db that was found, because it has multiple users.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.success_msg
documentation:
Success message, for configuration-only run.

Note: This is immensely helpful; I use it just about every time I do a
  manual test run.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy_messages.success_msg_automate_all
documentation: Success message, when using --automate-all.

modules.name: dsd_flyio.deploy
documentation:
Manages all Fly.io-specific aspects of the deployment process.

Notes:
- Internal references to Fly.io will almost always be flyio. Public references, may be fly_io.
- self.deployed_project_name and self.app_name are identical. The first is used in the
  simple_deploy CLI, but Fly refers to "apps" in their docs. This redundancy makes it
  easier to code Fly CLI commands.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy.simple_deploy_automate_all_supported
documentation: Specify whether --automate-all is supported on the specified platform.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy.simple_deploy_get_automate_all_msg
documentation: Get platform-specific confirmation message for --automate-all flag.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy.simple_deploy_get_platform_name
documentation:
Return the name of the platform that's being deployed to.

DEV: Consider returning a config object if there ends up being more info to share
back to core.

functions.name: dsd_flyio.deploy.simple_deploy_deploy
documentation: Carry out platform-specific deployment steps.

modules.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer
documentation:
Manages all Fly.io-specific aspects of the deployment process.

Notes:
- Internal references to Fly.io will almost always be flyio. Public references, such as
  the --platform argument, will be fly_io.
- self.deployed_project_name and self.app_name are identical. The first is used in the
  simple_deploy CLI, but Fly refers to "apps" in their docs. This redundancy makes it
  easier to code Fly CLI commands.

classes.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer.deploy
documentation: Coordinate the overall configuration and deployment.
--- Public methods ---

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._validate_platform
documentation:
Make sure the local environment and project supports deployment to Fly.io.

Make sure CLI is installed, and user is authenticated. Make sure necessary
resources have been created and identified, and that we have the user's
permission to use those resources.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If we find any reason deployment won't work.
--- Helper methods for deploy() ---

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._prep_automate_all
documentation: Take any further actions needed if using automate_all.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_env_vars
documentation: Set Fly.io-specific environment variables.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_on_flyio
documentation:
Set a secret, ON_FLYIO. This is used in settings.py to apply
deployment-specific settings.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_debug
documentation:
Set a secret, DEBUG=FALSE. This is used in settings.py to apply
deployment-specific settings.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_dockerfile
documentation:
Add a minimal dockerfile.

Different dependency management systems need different Dockerfiles. We could
send an argument to the template to dynamically generate the appropriate
dockerfile, but that makes the template *much* harder to read and reason about.
It's much nicer to keep that logic in here, and have a couple clean templates
that read almost as easily as the final dockerfiles that are generated for each
dependency management system.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_dockerignore
documentation:
Add a dockerignore file, based on user's local project environmnet.
Ignore virtual environment dir, system-specific cruft, and IDE cruft.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_flytoml
documentation: Add a minimal fly.toml file.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._modify_settings
documentation: Add platformsh-specific settings.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_requirements
documentation: Add requirements for deploying to Fly.io.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._conclude_automate_all
documentation:
Finish automating the push to Fly.io.

- Commit all changes.
- Call `fly deploy`.
- Call `fly apps open`, and grab URL.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._show_success_message
documentation:
After a successful run, show a message about what to do next.

Describe ongoing approach of commit, push, migrate.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._set_secret
documentation:
Set a secret on Fly, if it's not already set.

DEV: Do we need to say that it's already set, and get confirmation to change
value? (Only needed if it's not set to same value.)

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._build_dockerignore
documentation: Build the contents of the dockerignore file.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_flyio_settings
documentation: Check to see if a Fly.io settings block already exists.
--- Helper methods for _validate_platform() ---

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._validate_cli
documentation: Make sure the Fly.io CLI is installed, and user is authenticated.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._get_deployed_project_name
documentation:
Get the Fly.io project name.

Parse the output of `fly apps list`, and look for apps that have not
been deployed yet. Note there's some ambiguity between the use of
"project name" and "app name". This comes from usage in both Django
and target platforms. Also note that database apps can't be easily
distinguished from other apps.

During automated runs, creates a new Fly app if there isn't one we can use.

User interactions:
- If one app found, prompts user to confirm correct app.
- If multiple apps found, prompts user to select correct one.

Sets:
    str: self.app_name

Returns:
    str: The deployed project name (self.app_name). Empty string if
    using --automate-all.

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If deployed project name can't be found.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._get_undeployed_projects
documentation: Identify fly apps that have not yet been deployed to.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._select_project_name
documentation: Select the correct project to deploy to.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._create_flyio_app
documentation:
Create a new Fly.io app.

Assumes caller already checked for automate_all, and that a suitable app to
deploy to is not already available.

Sets:
    str: self.app_name

Returns:
    str: self.app_name

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: if an app can't be created.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._create_db
documentation:
Create a remote database.

An appropriate db should not already exist. We tell people to create
an app, and then we take care of everything else. We create a db with
the name app_name-db.

We'll look for a db with that name, for example in case someone has already run
deploy, but only gotten partway through the deployment process. If one
exists, we'll ask if we should use it. Otherwise, we'll just create a new db for
the app.

Sets:
    str: self.db_name

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If can't create a new db, or don't get
    permission to use existing db with matching name.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._get_region
documentation:
Get the region nearest to the user.

Notes:
- V1 `fly apps create` automatically configured a region for the app.
- In V2, an app doesn't have a region; it's really a container for machines,
  which do have regions.
- We need a region to create a db.
- `fly postgres create` only prompts for a region, there's no -q or -y.
- `fly postgres create` does highlight nearest region.
- `fly platform regions` lists available regions, but doesn't identify nearest.
- Possible approach:
  - Default to sea just so deployments work for now. They'll be slow for people
    far from sea.
  - Full fix: Parse `fly platform regions`, present list, ask user to select
    nearest region.

Current approach:
- This forum post: https://community.fly.io/t/feature-requests-region-latency-tests/968/6
- Leads to this tool: https://liveview-counter.fly.dev/
- It identifies the region with lowest latency, ie "Connected to iad".
- Solution: request this page, parse for that phrase, select region.
- Return 'sea' if this doesn't work.

Returns:
    str: Region with lowest latency for user.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_db_exists
documentation:
Check if a postgres db already exists that should be used with this app.

Returns:
    bool: True if appropriate db found; False if not found.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._manage_existing_db
documentation:
Figure out what to do with an existing db whose name matches app.

Returns:
    None: If we can use and configure existing db.

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If we can't use existing db, or fail to configure
    it correctly.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_db_attached
documentation:
Check if the db that was found is attached to this app.

Database is considered attached to this app it has a user with the same
name as the app, using underscores instead of hyphens. This is the default
behavior if you create a new app, then a new db, then attach the db to that app.

Sets:
    list: self.db_users, which can be used in messages to the user.

Returns:
    bool: True if attached to this app, False if not attached.

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If this db has users in addtion to the default db
    users and a user corresponding to this app, we raise an error.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._confirm_use_attached_db
documentation:
Confirm it's okay to use db that's already attached to this app.

Returns:
    None: If confirmation granted.

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If confirmation denied.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._confirm_use_unattached_db
documentation:
Confirm it's okay to use db whose name matches this app, but hasn't
been attached to this app.

If confirmation given, calls _attach_db().

Sets:
    str: self.db_name

Returns:
    None: If confirmation given.

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If confirmation denied.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._confirm_create_db
documentation:
Confirm the user wants a database created on their behalf.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If not confirmed.

methods.name: dsd_flyio.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._attach_db
documentation: Attach the database to the app.

Perform the initial deployment to Fly.io

If --automate-all is used, carry out an actual deployment.
If not, do all configuration work so the user only has to commit changes, and call
`fly deploy`.
